page: Microsoft Azure E128ds_v6 is an Edsv6-family virtual machine configured with 128 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 architecture. The system runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and features an Intel processor. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

This server type includes 7559 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The high local storage capacity combined with the memory-to-core ratio positions this instance for workloads requiring significant temporary storage and memory space.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E128ds_v6 offers high resource density, combining a large memory footprint with substantial local storage on a single dedicated virtual machine. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive applications, large-scale databases, and enterprise workloads that require substantial local disk capacity and high memory-to-CPU ratios without the need for hardware acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E128ds_v6 is an Edsv6-family virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture, managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. The server includes 7559 GB of local storage and supports premium storage configurations, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This high-density resource allocation makes the instance suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large databases, and workloads requiring substantial local storage capacity.
